item.title: C# 异常处理：技术和最佳实践 | C# Exception Handling: Techniques and Best Practices
item.description:
在本综合指南中，我们将深入探讨 C# 异常处理的世界。 我们将涵盖从基础知识到高级技术的所有内容，以及帮助您编写干净、健壮和可维护代码的最佳实践。 那么，让我们开始吧！

在任何软件应用程序中，都可能出现导致错误或崩溃的意外情况。 为了保证流畅的用户体验和维护应用程序的稳定性，我们需要有效地处理这些异常。

通过实施适当的异常处理，您可以优雅地处理错误，确保您的应用程序不会崩溃并可以继续执行剩余的代码。

为什么异常处理很重要？

异常处理对于任何软件应用程序都是至关重要的，原因如下：

  • 它提高了应用程序的可靠性和稳定性。
  • 它通过提供有意义的错误消息和防止突然崩溃来增强用户体验。
  • 它使您的代码更易于维护和调试。

了解 C# 中的异常类

在深入了解实现细节之前，让我们先了解一下 C# 异常处理的基础——Exception 类。

系统异常类

在 C# 中，异常是从System.Exception类继承的对象。 此类提供了许多属性和方法，可帮助您收集有关异常的信息，例如错误消息、来源和堆栈跟踪。

常用异常类

一些最常用的异常类是：

  • System.NullReferenceException
  • System.ArgumentException
  • System.DivideByZeroException
  • System.IndexOutOfRangeException
  • System.IO.FileNotFoundException

实现 C# 异常处理

现在我们对Exception类有了基本的了解，让我们看一下用于在 C# 中实现异常处理的各种结构。

可能抛出异常的代码包裹在try块中，而catch块包含发生异常时将执行的代码。

下面是一个使用try-catch块的简单示例：

 try { // Code that might throw an exception int result = 10 / 0; } catch (DivideByZeroException ex) { // Handle the exception Console.WriteLine("Error: " + ex.Message); }

最后块

它包含将始终执行的代码，无论是否发生异常。

 try { // Code that might throw an exception } catch (Exception ex) { // Handle the exception } finally { // This code will always execute Console.WriteLine("Finally block executed."); }

嵌套的 Try-Catch 块

您还可以相互嵌套try-catch块以在多个级别处理异常。 当您希望代码的不同部分处理特定异常时，此技术很有用。

 try { // Outer try block try { // Inner try block } catch (ArgumentException ex) { // Handle ArgumentException } } catch (Exception ex) { // Handle all other exceptions }

抛出语句

当您想要将异常传播到调用堆栈或为特定场景创建自定义异常时，这会很有帮助。

 try { // Check for an invalid condition if (someCondition) { throw new InvalidOperationException("Invalid operation."); } } catch (InvalidOperationException ex) { // Handle the exception Console.WriteLine("Error: " + ex.Message); }

为确保您的异常处理代码高效且可维护，请遵循以下最佳实践：

使用特定异常

始终捕获最具体的异常。 这种方法可以帮助您以更有针对性和更有意义的方式处理不同的异常。

 try { // Code that might throw an exception } catch (FileNotFoundException ex) { // Handle FileNotFoundException } catch (IOException ex) { // Handle other IO exceptions } catch (Exception ex) { // Handle all other exceptions }

不要直接捕获 System.Exception

避免直接捕获System.Exception类，因为它可以掩盖其他重要的异常。 相反，如上例所示捕获特定异常。

避免捕获您无法处理的异常

只捕获您可以处理或从中恢复的异常。 如果您无法在特定代码块内处理异常，请让它向上传播到可以适当处理的调用堆栈。

使用“使用”语句进行清理

使用实现IDisposable接口的资源（例如文件流、数据库连接或网络套接字）时，请使用using语句确保正确清理。

 using (StreamReader reader = new StreamReader("file.txt")) { // Code that uses the StreamReader } // The StreamReader is automatically disposed when the using block exits

有效地记录异常

记录异常详细信息，包括错误消息、来源和堆栈跟踪，以帮助调试和分析。 确保根据异常的严重程度在适当的级别（例如，错误、警告或信息）记录异常。

通过组合这些技术，您可以为您的应用程序创建一个健壮且可维护的错误处理策略。 让我们更详细地探讨其中一些技术。

错误处理程序是一段集中式代码，用于处理整个应用程序中的异常。 这可能包括记录日志、显示错误消息或根据异常类型采取其他操作。 您可以创建一个单独的类或方法来处理异常，并在异常发生时调用它。

例如，您可以创建一个ErrorHandler类来记录异常并向用户显示有意义的错误消息：

 public static class ErrorHandler { public static void HandleException(Exception ex) { // Log the exception LogException(ex); // Display a user-friendly error message ShowErrorMessage(ex); } private static void LogException(Exception ex) { // Use your preferred logging framework to log the exception details Console.WriteLine("Error: " + ex.Message); } private static void ShowErrorMessage(Exception ex) { // Show a user-friendly error message, eg, using a message box or a custom UI element Console.WriteLine("An error occurred: " + ex.Message); } }

然后，您可以从catch块中调用HandleException方法：

 try { // Code that might throw an exception } catch (Exception ex) { ErrorHandler.HandleException(ex); }

通过将错误代码分配给自定义异常类，您可以提供有关异常的更多信息，并使跟踪和处理特定错误变得更加容易。 例如，您可以创建一个带有ErrorCode属性的自定义异常类：

 public class CustomException : ApplicationException { public int ErrorCode { get; } public CustomException(string message, int errorCode) : base(message) { ErrorCode = errorCode; } }

然后，您可以抛出并捕获此自定义异常，使用ErrorCode属性来确定适当的操作：

 try { // Throw a custom exception with an error code throw new CustomException("Invalid operation.", 1001); } catch (CustomException ex) { // Handle the exception based on the error code Console.WriteLine("Error Code: " + ex.ErrorCode + ", Message: " + ex.Message); }

流程异常是在特定流程或操作中发生的异常。 您可以通过使用try-catch块并记录异常详细信息以供进一步分析来处理进程异常。 此外，您可以使用自定义异常类来提供有关失败的进程或操作的更多上下文。

例如，考虑一个可能抛出IOException文件处理操作。 您可以创建一个自定义FileProcessingException类，其中包含有关正在处理的文件的附加信息：

 public class FileProcessingException : IOException { public string FileName { get; } public FileProcessingException(string message, string fileName) : base(message) { FileName = fileName; } }

当您在文件处理过程中遇到IOException时，您可以将其包装在FileProcessingException中并添加文件名：

 try { // Code that processes a file and may throw an IOException } catch (IOException ex) { // Wrap the IOException in a FileProcessingException with the file name throw new FileProcessingException("Error processing file: " + fileName, fileName); }

通过以这种方式处理流程异常，您可以提供有关错误的更多上下文，并使诊断和修复应用程序中的问题变得更加容易。

让我们探索一些可以帮助您增强 C# 异常处理能力的高级技术。

自定义异常类

您可以通过从System.Exception类或其派生类之一继承来创建自定义异常类。 这使您可以根据应用程序的要求创建特定的异常。

 public class CustomException : ApplicationException { public CustomException(string message) : base(message) { } }

异常过滤器

异常过滤器允许您根据特定条件捕获异常。 它们使您能够编写更有针对性和更高效的异常处理代码。

 try { // Code that might throw an exception } catch (ArgumentException ex) when (ex.ParamName == "param1") { // Handle exception for param1 } catch (ArgumentException ex) when (ex.ParamName == "param2") { // Handle exception for param2 }

聚合异常

聚合异常用于将多个异常包装成一个异常。 这在处理可能同时发生多个异常的异步或并行代码时特别有用。

 try { // Code that might throw multiple exceptions } catch (AggregateException ex) { // Handle multiple exceptions foreach (Exception innerEx in ex.InnerExceptions) { Console.WriteLine("Error: " + innerEx.Message); }

除了 C# 异常处理最佳实践之外，还有一些在 .NET 生态系统中处理异常的一般最佳实践。 这些实践适用于所有 .NET 语言和平台，确保采用一致且有效的方法来处理错误。 让我们更深入地研究这些最佳实践。

集中异常处理

实施集中式异常处理机制，以在整个应用程序中一致地处理异常。 这可以使用捕获和处理未处理异常的全局异常处理程序或中间件来完成。

下面是使用AppDomain.UnhandledException事件处理 .NET 控制台应用程序中未处理的异常的示例：

 public class Program { public static void Main() { // Register a global exception handler AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException += HandleUnhandledException; // Code that might throw an exception } private static void HandleUnhandledException(object sender, UnhandledExceptionEventArgs e) { // Log the exception and perform necessary cleanup Console.WriteLine("Unhandled exception: " + e.ExceptionObject.ToString()); } }

在 ASP.NET Core 应用程序中，您可以使用中间件在全局级别处理异常。 例如，您可以创建自定义异常处理中间件：

 public class ExceptionHandlingMiddleware { private readonly RequestDelegate _next; public ExceptionHandlingMiddleware(RequestDelegate next) { _next = next; } public async Task InvokeAsync(HttpContext context) { try { await _next(context); } catch (Exception ex) { // Handle the exception Console.WriteLine("Unhandled exception: " + ex.Message); } } }

然后在Startup类的Configure方法中注册：

 public void Configure(IApplicationBuilder app, IWebHostEnvironment env) { // Add the exception handling middleware app.UseMiddleware<ExceptionHandlingMiddleware>(); // Other middleware registrations and configuration }

在所有 .NET 语言和平台上应用相同的错误处理最佳实践。 这些做法包括：

  • 使用特定的异常而不是通用的Exception类来更有效地处理错误。
  • 记录带有详细信息的异常，例如消息、源和堆栈跟踪，以帮助调试和分析。
  • 实施集中式错误处理机制以在整个应用程序中一致地处理异常。

在 ASP.NET 应用程序中，您可以使用 Global.asax 或中间件中的Application_Error处理各种级别的异常，例如控制器级别、操作级别，甚至全局。 以下是使用HandleError属性在操作级别处理异常的示例：

 [HandleError(ExceptionType = typeof(NullReferenceException), View = "NullReferenceError")] public ActionResult Index() { // Code that might throw a NullReferenceException }

对于 ASP.NET Core 应用程序中的全局异常处理，您可以使用UseExceptionHandler中间件。 以下是在Startup类中配置它的方法：

 public void Configure(IApplicationBuilder app, IWebHostEnvironment env) { app.UseExceptionHandler(errorApp => { errorApp.Run(async context => { var exceptionHandler = context.Features.Get<IExceptionHandlerFeature>(); // Log the exception Console.WriteLine("Unhandled exception: " + exceptionHandler.Error.Message); // Display a custom error page or error message to the user context.Response.StatusCode = 500; await context.Response.WriteAsync("An error occurred. Please try again later."); }); }); // Other middleware registrations and configuration }

此外，考虑使用自定义错误页面来提供更好的用户体验。 在 ASP.NET Core 中，您可以使用UseStatusCodePagesWithReExecute中间件来显示基于 HTTP 状态代码的自定义错误页面：

 public void Configure(IApplicationBuilder app, IWebHostEnvironment env) { app.UseStatusCodePagesWithReExecute("/Error/{0}"); // Other middleware registrations and configuration }

通过遵循这些最佳实践，您可以确保您的 .NET 应用程序有效且一致地处理异常，从而产生更健壮和可维护的代码。

在 C# 代码中使用try-catch块时，必须遵循最佳实践以确保有效且可维护的异常处理。 让我们更详细地探讨这些最佳实践，以及高级技术和代码示例。

如何在 C# 中处理异常

要在 C# 中有效地处理异常，请遵循以下准则：

  • 确定您的代码可能抛出的特定异常并单独捕获它们。
  • 使用自定义异常类来提供有关错误的更多上下文，并使诊断和修复应用程序中的问题变得更加容易。
  • 实施集中式异常处理机制，以在整个应用程序中一致地处理异常。
  • 使用异常过滤器根据特定条件捕获异常，使您能够编写更有针对性和更高效的异常处理代码。

下面是一个使用带有try-catch块的异常过滤器的例子：

 try { // Code that might throw an exception } catch (IOException ex) when (ex is FileNotFoundException) { // Handle FileNotFoundException } catch (IOException ex) { // Handle other IO exceptions }

尝试捕捉最佳实践 C#

使用try-catch块时，请牢记以下最佳实践：

  • 保持try块尽可能小，重点关注可能抛出异常的代码。 这使您的代码更具可读性和更易于维护。
  • 不要使用空的catch块，因为它们可以隐藏重要的异常。 相反，始终提供某种形式的异常处理，例如记录异常或向用户显示错误消息。
  • 使用finally块来清理资源并确保执行任何必要的清理代码，即使发生异常也是如此。

下面是使用finally块关闭文件流的示例：

 FileStream fileStream = null; try { fileStream = new FileStream("file.txt", FileMode.Open); // Code that uses the file stream } catch (Exception ex) { // Handle the exception Console.WriteLine("Error: " + ex.Message); } finally { // Close the file stream if it's not null fileStream?.Close(); }

记录异常时，请遵循以下最佳实践：

  • 记录异常详细信息，包括消息、源和堆栈跟踪，以帮助调试和分析。
  • 使用集中式日志记录框架（例如 NLog 或 log4net）来管理您的日志输出。 这使您可以在整个应用程序中保持一致的日志记录，并轻松配置日志输出目标和格式。
  • 根据异常的严重性和对应用程序的影响，在适当的级别记录异常。 例如，导致应用程序崩溃的严重异常应该记录在“错误”级别，而不太严重的异常可以记录在“警告”或“信息”级别。

下面是使用 NLog 记录异常的示例：

 private static readonly NLog.Logger Logger = NLog.LogManager.GetCurrentClassLogger(); try { // Code that might throw an exception } catch (Exception ex) { // Log the exception as an error Logger.Error(ex, "An error occurred: {0}", ex.Message); }

通过遵循这些最佳实践和高级技术，您可以确保您的 C# 应用程序有效地处理异常，从而生成更健壮和可维护的代码。

结论

在本综合指南中，我们探索了 C# 异常处理的世界，从基础知识到高级技术和最佳实践。 通过遵循这些准则并在您的代码中实施有效的异常处理，您可以创建更健壮、可维护且用户友好的应用程序。

关键要点

  • 了解 C# 中异常处理的重要性以及System.Exception类的作用。
  • 使用try-catch块、 finally块和嵌套的异常处理结构来管理代码中的异常。
  • 遵循 C# 异常处理最佳实践，例如有效使用特定异常和记录异常详细信息。
  • 实施高级异常处理技术，例如自定义异常类、异常过滤器和聚合异常。
  • 在整个应用程序堆栈中应用 .NET 异常处理最佳实践。

进一步阅读

要了解有关 C# 异常处理和相关概念的更多信息，请查看以下资源：



原文:

In this comprehensive guide, we’ll dive deep into the world of C# exception handling. We’ll cover everything from the basics to advanced techniques, along with best practices to help you write clean, robust, and maintainable code. So, let’s get started!

Introduction to C# Exception Handling

In any software application, unexpected situations may arise that can lead to errors or crashes. To ensure a smooth user experience and maintain application stability, we need to handle these exceptions effectively.

What is Exception Handling in C#?

Exception handling in C# is a mechanism that allows you to detect and manage runtime anomalies within your code. By implementing proper exception handling, you can handle errors gracefully, ensuring that your application doesn’t crash and can continue executing the remaining code.

Why is Exception Handling Important?

Exception handling is vital for any software application for the following reasons:

  • It improves the reliability and stability of your application.
  • It enhances the user experience by providing meaningful error messages and preventing abrupt crashes.
  • It makes your code more maintainable and easier to debug.

Understanding the Exception Class in C#

Before diving into the implementation details, let’s understand the foundation of C# exception handling—the Exception class.

The System.Exception Class

In C#, exceptions are objects that inherit from the System.Exception class. This class provides a number of properties and methods that help you gather information about the exception, such as the error message, the source, and the stack trace.

Commonly Used Exception Classes

C# provides several built-in exception classes that cater to specific error scenarios. Some of the most commonly used exception classes are:

  • System.NullReferenceException
  • System.ArgumentException
  • System.DivideByZeroException
  • System.IndexOutOfRangeException
  • System.IO.FileNotFoundException

Implementing C# Exception Handling

Now that we have a basic understanding of the Exception class, let’s look at the various constructs used to implement exception handling in C#.

The Try-Catch Block

The try-catch block is the fundamental construct used to handle exceptions. The code that might throw an exception is wrapped within the try block, while the catch block contains the code that will be executed when an exception occurs.

Here’s a simple example of using a try-catch block:

try
{ // Code that might throw an exception int result = 10 / 0;
}
catch (DivideByZeroException ex)
{ // Handle the exception Console.WriteLine("Error: " + ex.Message);
}

The Finally Block

The finally block is an optional block that can be added after the catch block. It contains code that will always be executed, whether an exception occurs or not.

try
{ // Code that might throw an exception
}
catch (Exception ex)
{ // Handle the exception
}
finally
{ // This code will always execute Console.WriteLine("Finally block executed.");
}

Nested Try-Catch Blocks

You can also nest try-catch blocks within each other to handle exceptions at multiple levels. This technique is useful when you want different parts of your code to handle specific exceptions.

try
{ // Outer try block try { // Inner try block } catch (ArgumentException ex) { // Handle ArgumentException }
}
catch (Exception ex)
{ // Handle all other exceptions
}

The Throw Statement

The throw statement is used to raise an exception manually. This can be helpful when you want to propagate an exception up the call stack or create custom exceptions for specific scenarios.

try
{ // Check for an invalid condition if (someCondition) { throw new InvalidOperationException("Invalid operation."); }
}
catch (InvalidOperationException ex)
{ // Handle the exception Console.WriteLine("Error: " + ex.Message);
}

C# Exception Handling Best Practices

To ensure that your exception handling code is efficient and maintainable, follow these best practices:

Using Specific Exceptions

Always catch the most specific exception possible. This approach helps you handle different exceptions in a more targeted and meaningful way.

try
{ // Code that might throw an exception
}
catch (FileNotFoundException ex)
{ // Handle FileNotFoundException
}
catch (IOException ex)
{ // Handle other IO exceptions
}
catch (Exception ex)
{ // Handle all other exceptions
}

Do Not Catch System.Exception Directly

Avoid catching the System.Exception class directly, as it can mask other important exceptions. Instead, catch specific exceptions as shown in the previous example.

Avoid Catching Exceptions You Cannot Handle

Only catch exceptions that you can handle or recover from. If you cannot handle an exception within a certain block of code, let it propagate up the call stack where it can be handled appropriately.

Use ‘Using’ Statement for Cleanup

When working with resources that implement the IDisposable interface (such as file streams, database connections, or network sockets), use the using statement to ensure proper cleanup.

using (StreamReader reader = new StreamReader("file.txt"))
{ // Code that uses the StreamReader
}
// The StreamReader is automatically disposed when the using block exits

Logging Exceptions Effectively

Log exception details, including the error message, source, and stack trace, to help with debugging and analysis. Make sure to log exceptions at the appropriate level (e.g., error, warning, or info) based on their severity.

C# Error Handling Techniques

Error handling in C# involves various techniques that help you manage and handle exceptions effectively. By combining these techniques, you can create a robust and maintainable error handling strategy for your application. Let’s explore some of these techniques in more detail.

C# Error Handler

An error handler is a centralized piece of code that deals with exceptions across your application. This can include logging, displaying error messages, or taking other actions based on the exception type. You can create a separate class or method to handle exceptions and call it whenever an exception occurs.

For example, you can create an ErrorHandler class that logs exceptions and displays meaningful error messages to the user:

public static class ErrorHandler
{ public static void HandleException(Exception ex) { // Log the exception LogException(ex); // Display a user-friendly error message ShowErrorMessage(ex); } private static void LogException(Exception ex) { // Use your preferred logging framework to log the exception details Console.WriteLine("Error: " + ex.Message); } private static void ShowErrorMessage(Exception ex) { // Show a user-friendly error message, e.g., using a message box or a custom UI element Console.WriteLine("An error occurred: " + ex.Message); }
}

You can then call the HandleException method from your catch blocks:

try
{ // Code that might throw an exception
}
catch (Exception ex)
{ ErrorHandler.HandleException(ex);
}

C# Exception Error Code

By assigning error codes to custom exception classes, you can provide more information about the exception and make it easier to track and handle specific errors. For example, you can create a custom exception class with an ErrorCode property:

public class CustomException : ApplicationException
{ public int ErrorCode { get; } public CustomException(string message, int errorCode) : base(message) { ErrorCode = errorCode; }
}

You can then throw and catch this custom exception, using the ErrorCode property to determine the appropriate action:

try
{ // Throw a custom exception with an error code throw new CustomException("Invalid operation.", 1001);
}
catch (CustomException ex)
{ // Handle the exception based on the error code Console.WriteLine("Error Code: " + ex.ErrorCode + ", Message: " + ex.Message);
}

C# Process Exception

A process exception is an exception that occurs within a specific process or operation. You can handle process exceptions by using try-catch blocks and logging the exception details for further analysis. In addition, you can use custom exception classes to provide more context about the process or operation that failed.

For example, consider a file processing operation that may throw an IOException. You can create a custom FileProcessingException class that includes additional information about the file being processed:

public class FileProcessingException : IOException
{ public string FileName { get; } public FileProcessingException(string message, string fileName) : base(message) { FileName = fileName; }
}

When you encounter an IOException during file processing, you can wrap it in a FileProcessingException and add the file name:

try
{ // Code that processes a file and may throw an IOException
}
catch (IOException ex)
{ // Wrap the IOException in a FileProcessingException with the file name throw new FileProcessingException("Error processing file: " + fileName, fileName);
}

By handling process exceptions in this way, you can provide more context about the error and make it easier to diagnose and fix issues in your application.

Advanced Exception Handling in C#

Let’s explore some advanced techniques that can help you enhance your exception handling capabilities in C#.

Custom Exception Classes

You can create custom exception classes by inheriting from the System.Exception class or one of its derived classes. This allows you to create specific exceptions tailored to your application’s requirements.

public class CustomException : ApplicationException
{ public CustomException(string message) : base(message) { }
}

Exception Filters

Exception filters allow you to catch exceptions based on specific conditions. They enable you to write more targeted and efficient exception handling code.

try
{ // Code that might throw an exception
}
catch (ArgumentException ex) when (ex.ParamName == "param1")
{ // Handle exception for param1
}
catch (ArgumentException ex) when (ex.ParamName == "param2")
{ // Handle exception for param2
}

Aggregate Exceptions

Aggregate exceptions are used to wrap multiple exceptions into a single exception. This is particularly useful when dealing with asynchronous or parallel code where multiple exceptions can occur simultaneously.

try
{
// Code that might throw multiple exceptions
}
catch (AggregateException ex)
{
// Handle multiple exceptions
foreach (Exception innerEx in ex.InnerExceptions)
{
Console.WriteLine("Error: " + innerEx.Message);
}

.NET Exception Handling Best Practices

In addition to the C# exception handling best practices, there are some general best practices for handling exceptions in the .NET ecosystem. These practices apply across all .NET languages and platforms, ensuring a consistent and effective approach to error handling. Let’s dive deeper into these best practices.

Centralized Exception Handling

Implement a centralized exception handling mechanism to handle exceptions consistently across your application. This can be done using global exception handlers or middleware that capture and handle unhandled exceptions.

Here’s an example of using the AppDomain.UnhandledException event to handle unhandled exceptions in a .NET console application:

public class Program
{ public static void Main() { // Register a global exception handler AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException += HandleUnhandledException; // Code that might throw an exception } private static void HandleUnhandledException(object sender, UnhandledExceptionEventArgs e) { // Log the exception and perform necessary cleanup Console.WriteLine("Unhandled exception: " + e.ExceptionObject.ToString()); }
}

In an ASP.NET Core application, you can use middleware to handle exceptions at the global level. For example, you can create a custom exception handling middleware:

public class ExceptionHandlingMiddleware
{ private readonly RequestDelegate _next; public ExceptionHandlingMiddleware(RequestDelegate next) { _next = next; } public async Task InvokeAsync(HttpContext context) { try { await _next(context); } catch (Exception ex) { // Handle the exception Console.WriteLine("Unhandled exception: " + ex.Message); } }
}

And then register it in the Configure method of the Startup class:

public void Configure(IApplicationBuilder app, IWebHostEnvironment env)
{ // Add the exception handling middleware app.UseMiddleware<ExceptionHandlingMiddleware>(); // Other middleware registrations and configuration
}

.NET Error Handling Best Practices

Apply the same error handling best practices across all .NET languages and platforms. These practices include:

  • Using specific exceptions instead of the generic Exception class to handle errors more effectively.
  • Logging exceptions with detailed information, such as the message, source, and stack trace, to help with debugging and analysis.
  • Implementing a centralized error handling mechanism to handle exceptions consistently across your application.

Exception Handling in ASP.NET

In ASP.NET applications, you can handle exceptions at various levels, such as at the controller level, action level, or even globally, using Application_Error in Global.asax or middleware. Here’s an example of handling exceptions at the action level using the HandleError attribute:

[HandleError(ExceptionType = typeof(NullReferenceException), View = "NullReferenceError")]
public ActionResult Index()
{ // Code that might throw a NullReferenceException
}

For global exception handling in an ASP.NET Core application, you can use the UseExceptionHandler middleware. Here’s how to configure it in the Startup class:

public void Configure(IApplicationBuilder app, IWebHostEnvironment env)
{ app.UseExceptionHandler(errorApp => { errorApp.Run(async context => { var exceptionHandler = context.Features.Get<IExceptionHandlerFeature>(); // Log the exception Console.WriteLine("Unhandled exception: " + exceptionHandler.Error.Message); // Display a custom error page or error message to the user context.Response.StatusCode = 500; await context.Response.WriteAsync("An error occurred. Please try again later."); }); }); // Other middleware registrations and configuration
}

Additionally, consider using custom error pages to provide a better user experience. In ASP.NET Core, you can use the UseStatusCodePagesWithReExecute middleware to display custom error pages based on the HTTP status code:

public void Configure(IApplicationBuilder app, IWebHostEnvironment env)
{ app.UseStatusCodePagesWithReExecute("/Error/{0}"); // Other middleware registrations and configuration
}

By following these best practices, you can ensure that your .NET applications handle exceptions effectively and consistently, resulting in more robust and maintainable code.

C# Try Catch Best Practices

When using try-catch blocks in your C# code, it’s essential to follow best practices to ensure effective and maintainable exception handling. Let’s explore these best practices in more detail, along with advanced techniques and code examples.

How to Handle Exceptions in C#

To handle exceptions effectively in C#, follow these guidelines:

  • Identify the specific exceptions that your code might throw and catch them individually.
  • Use custom exception classes to provide more context about the error and make it easier to diagnose and fix issues in your application.
  • Implement a centralized exception handling mechanism to handle exceptions consistently across your application.
  • Use exception filters to catch exceptions based on specific conditions, enabling you to write more targeted and efficient exception handling code.

Here’s an example of using exception filters with a try-catch block:

try
{ // Code that might throw an exception
}
catch (IOException ex) when (ex is FileNotFoundException)
{ // Handle FileNotFoundException
}
catch (IOException ex)
{ // Handle other IO exceptions
}

Try Catch Best Practices C#

When working with try-catch blocks, keep the following best practices in mind:

  • Keep the try block as small as possible, focusing on the code that may throw an exception. This makes your code more readable and easier to maintain.
  • Do not use empty catch blocks, as they can hide important exceptions. Instead, always provide some form of exception handling, such as logging the exception or displaying an error message to the user.
  • Use the finally block to clean up resources and ensure that any necessary cleanup code is executed, even if an exception occurs.

Here’s an example of using the finally block to close a file stream:

FileStream fileStream = null; try
{ fileStream = new FileStream("file.txt", FileMode.Open); // Code that uses the file stream
}
catch (Exception ex)
{ // Handle the exception Console.WriteLine("Error: " + ex.Message);
}
finally
{ // Close the file stream if it's not null fileStream?.Close();
}

C# Exception Logging Best Practices

When logging exceptions, follow these best practices:

  • Log the exception details, including the message, source, and stack trace, to help with debugging and analysis.
  • Use a centralized logging framework, such as NLog or log4net, to manage your log output. This allows you to maintain consistent logging across your application and easily configure log output destinations and formats.
  • Log exceptions at the appropriate level, based on their severity and impact on the application. For example, critical exceptions that cause your application to crash should be logged at the “Error” level, while less severe exceptions can be logged at the “Warning” or “Info” levels.

Here’s an example of using NLog to log exceptions:

private static readonly NLog.Logger Logger = NLog.LogManager.GetCurrentClassLogger(); try
{ // Code that might throw an exception
}
catch (Exception ex)
{ // Log the exception as an error Logger.Error(ex, "An error occurred: {0}", ex.Message);
}

By following these best practices and advanced techniques, you can ensure that your C# applications handle exceptions effectively, leading to more robust and maintainable code.

Conclusion

In this comprehensive guide, we’ve explored the world of C# exception handling, from the basics to advanced techniques and best practices. By following these guidelines and implementing effective exception handling in your code, you can create more robust, maintainable, and user-friendly applications.

Key Takeaways

  • Understand the importance of exception handling in C# and the role of the System.Exception class.
  • Use try-catch blocks, finally blocks, and nested exception handling structures to manage exceptions in your code.
  • Follow C# exception handling best practices, such as using specific exceptions and logging exception details effectively.
  • Implement advanced exception handling techniques, such as custom exception classes, exception filters, and aggregate exceptions.
  • Apply .NET exception handling best practices across your entire application stack.

Further Reading

To learn more about C# exception handling and related concepts, check out the following resources:

item.title: 使用 GitHub Actions 在 DigitalOcean 应用程序平台上部署 React 应用程序 | Deploying a React App on DigitalOcean App Platform Using GitHub Actions
item.description:
使用 GitHub Actions 可以简化在 DigitalOcean 应用程序平台上部署 React 应用程序。 在本文中，我们将完成设置部署工作流和使用 GitHub Actions 工作流文件自动部署的过程。

先决条件：
在我们开始之前，请确保您拥有 DigitalOcean Personal Access Token。 如果您没有，请按照 DigitalOcean 提供的说明创建令牌。

设置个人访问令牌：

  • 访问您的 GitHub 存储库并导航到存储库的 Secrets 设置。
  • 声明一个名为 DIGITALOCEAN_ACCESS_TOKEN 的新秘密并将其值设置为您的 DigitalOcean 个人访问令牌。 此令牌将在部署过程中安全存储和使用。

创建 GitHub 操作工作流程：

  • 创建新的 GitHub Action 工作流程文件或修改存储库中的现有工作流程文件。
  • 将以下工作流配置添加到文件中：
 name: Deploy app on: push: branches: - main jobs: build_and_deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - name: Checkout main branch uses: actions/checkout@v2 - name: Use Node.js uses: actions/setup-node@v1 with: node-version: '16.x' - name: Install dependencies run: yarn install - name: Test application run: yarn test - name: Deploy to DigitalOcean uses: digitalocean/app_action@main with: app_name: my_react_app token: ${{ secrets.DIGITALOCEAN_ACCESS_TOKEN }}

让我们来看看工作流程中的不同步骤：

签出主分支：此步骤确保工作流在存储库的主分支上运行。

设置 Node.js：此步骤为部署过程设置所需的 Node.js 环境。 我们指定所需的 Node.js 版本（在本例中为 16.x）。

安装依赖项：此步骤使用 yarn install 命令安装项目依赖项。 如果您使用不同的包管理器，请确保自定义它。

测试应用程序：此步骤使用 yarn test 命令为您的 React 应用程序运行测试。 如果您有不同的测试要求，请调整此步骤。

部署到 DigitalOcean：最后一步使用digitalocean/app_action操作来触发部署到 DigitalOcean App Platform。 将 app_name 参数设置为您在 DigitalOcean 上的 React 应用程序的名称。 令牌参数安全地获取先前设置的 DIGITALOCEAN_ACCESS_TOKEN 秘密。

按照本文中概述的步骤并使用提供的 GitHub Actions 工作流文件，您可以在 DigitalOcean App Platform 上自动部署 React 应用程序。 这种简化的流程让您可以专注于开发您的应用程序，同时确保高效可靠的部署

一旦暂停，_itsglover 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，_itsglover 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

取消发布后，_itsglover 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 _itsglover 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 _itsglover 的方法：

降低 _itsglover 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 _itsglover 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

 

Deploying a React app on the DigitalOcean App Platform can be streamlined using GitHub Actions. In this article, we'll go through the process of setting up a deployment workflow and automating the deployment using a GitHub Actions workflow file.

Prerequisites:
Before we begin, make sure you have a DigitalOcean Personal Access Token. If you don't have one, follow the instructions provided by DigitalOcean to create a token.

Setting Up the Personal Access Token:

  • Access your GitHub repository and navigate to the repository's Secrets settings.
  • Declare a new secret called DIGITALOCEAN_ACCESS_TOKEN and set its value to your DigitalOcean Personal Access Token. This token will be securely stored and used in the deployment process.

Creating the GitHub Action Workflow:

  • Create a new GitHub Action workflow file or modify an existing one in your repository.
  • Add the following workflow configuration to the file: 
name: Deploy app on: push: branches: - main jobs: build_and_deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - name: Checkout main branch uses: actions/checkout@v2 - name: Use Node.js uses: actions/setup-node@v1 with: node-version: '16.x' - name: Install dependencies run: yarn install - name: Test application run: yarn test - name: Deploy to DigitalOcean uses: digitalocean/app_action@main with: app_name: my_react_app token: ${{ secrets.DIGITALOCEAN_ACCESS_TOKEN }}

Let's go through the different steps in the workflow:

Checking out the main branch: This step ensures that the workflow operates on the main branch of your repository.

Setting up Node.js: This step sets up the required Node.js environment for the deployment process. We specify the desired Node.js version (in this case, 16.x).

Installing dependencies: This step installs the project dependencies using the yarn install command. Make sure to customize it if you're using a different package manager.

Testing the application: This step runs the tests for your React app using the yarn test command. Adjust this step if you have different testing requirements.

Deploying to DigitalOcean: The final step utilizes the digitalocean/app_action action to trigger the deployment to the DigitalOcean App Platform. Set the app_name parameter to the name of your React app on DigitalOcean. The token parameter fetches the previously set DIGITALOCEAN_ACCESS_TOKEN secret securely.

By following the steps outlined in this article and using the provided GitHub Actions workflow file, you can automate the deployment of your React app on the DigitalOcean App Platform. This streamlined process allows you to focus on developing your app while ensuring efficient and reliable deployments

Peter Kelvin Torver - Mar 31

Once suspended, _itsglover will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, _itsglover will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by _itsglover will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If _itsglover is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag _itsglover:

Make all posts by _itsglover less visible

_itsglover consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging _itsglover will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: _itsglover

item.title: Terraform - 了解生命周期块 | Terraform - Understanding the Lifecycle Block
item.description:
其中一项功能是生命周期配置块。

生命周期块提供了几个元参数来管理 Terraform 如何创建、更新、检查和删除资源。 在本文中，我们将深入探讨 Terraform 的生命周期块，并通过一些Microsoft Azure 资源示例来演示其用法。

了解生命周期块

注意：这篇文章是使用Terraform (v1.4.x)编写的

它是一个嵌套在资源块中的配置块，支持四个元参数：

 resource "provider_resource" "block" { // ... some resource configuration ... lifecycle { argument = value } }

生命周期块还有一个选项可以使用前置条件和后置条件配置自定义条件检查：

 resource "provider_resource" "block" { // ... some resource configuration ... lifecycle { precondition { condition = expression error_message = "error(string)" } postcondition { condition = expression error_message = "error(string)" } } }

稍后我们将仔细研究前置条件和后置条件，但让我们先看几个使用元参数的示例：

  • 防止破坏
  • 忽略更改
  • 替换触发者

参数类型：布尔值

在某些情况下，在创建新资源之前销毁资源可能会导致停机。 为了避免这种情况，我们可以将create_before_destroy设置为true 。 这在使用Azure 虚拟机或应用程序服务时特别有用，您希望将停机时间降至最低。

以下是 Azure 虚拟机的示例：

 resource "azurerm_virtual_machine" "example" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { create_before_destroy = true } }

在这种情况下，当需要更新但无法就地执行时，Terraform 将首先创建替代 VM，然后销毁旧 VM，从而减少潜在的停机时间。

参数类型：布尔值

将prevent_destroy设置为true是一种防止意外删除关键资源的保护措施。 如果您尝试销毁此类资源，Terraform 将返回错误并停止操作。 这在使用Azure SQL 数据库、存储帐户或任何包含重要数据的资源时非常有用。

以下是 Azure SQL 数据库的示例：

 resource "azurerm_sql_database" "example" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true } }

使用此配置，Terraform 将防止 SQL 数据库被意外破坏。

参数类型：属性名称列表

当你想在 Terraform 之外管理某些资源属性，或者当你想避免虚假差异时， ignore_changes参数很有用。

以下是 Azure 应用服务的示例：

 resource "azurerm_app_service" "example" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { ignore_changes = [ app_settings, // Ignore changes to app_settings attribute ] } }

例如，假设一个不同的团队管理一个App Services app_settings ，您可能正在配置该App Service ，但配置留给其他人，或者甚至可能是不同的自动化一起负责app_settings配置，而您没有希望 Terraform 恢复、干扰或可能删除这些设置。

在这种情况下，Terraform 将忽略对应用服务的app_settings的任何更改。

提示：您还可以使用一个特殊的值all ，一旦资源被供应，它将忽略所有设置。

 resource "azurerm_app_service" "example" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { ignore_changes = all } }

这将提供资源，Terraform 将忽略 Terraform 之外的任何后续配置。

参数类型：资源或属性引用列表

您只能在replace_triggered_by表达式中引用托管资源。 提供引用托管资源、实例或实例属性的表达式列表。

 resource "azurerm_sql_database" "example" { // ... other configuration ... } resource "azurerm_app_service" "example" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { replace_triggered_by = [ azurerm_sql_database.example.id, //Replace `azurerm_app_service` each time `azurerm_sql_database` id changes ] } }

本地值或输入变量等普通值没有自己的计划操作，但您可以通过将它们与terraform_data资源类型一起使用来使用类似资源的生命周期来处理它们。

自定义条件检查

您可以添加带有生命周期块的precondition和postcondition块，以指定关于资源和数据源如何运行的假设和保证。 以下示例创建了一个先决条件，用于检查 AMI 是否已正确配置。

 data "azurerm_mssql_server" "example" { // ... other configuration ... } resource "azurerm_mssql_database" "test" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { precondition { condition = data.azurerm_mssql_server.example.version == "12.0" error_message = "MSSQL server version incorrect (Needs to be version 12.0)." } postcondition { condition = self.transparent_data_encryption_enabled == true error_message = "The Database must have TDE enabled." } } }

注意：上面后postcondition块中的self 对象指的是正在评估的实例的属性（例如 MSSQL 数据库）。

您可以将验证检查作为生成数据的资源的postcondition ，或者作为使用数据的资源或输出值的precondition 。 要决定哪个最合适，请考虑支票是代表假设还是保证。

在我们上面的例子中：

  • 假设：使用preconditions验证正在创建的数据库位于版本为12.0的MSSQL 服务器上。
  • 保证：使用postcondition验证正在创建的MSSQL 数据库(SELF)已将transparent_data_encryption_enabled设置为true 。

结论

无论是防止关键资源的意外破坏、管理零停机更新，还是忽略对某些属性的更改，生命周期块都能为您提供所需的灵活性。 与往常一样，在部署到生产环境之前，一定要在非生产环境中测试这些配置，以确保它们按预期工作。 地球改造快乐！

我希望你喜欢这篇文章并学到了新东西。 作者

喜欢，分享，关注我：🐙 GitHub | 🐧 Twitter | 🐧推特| 👾 LinkedIn 👾领英



原文:

Terraform offers a range of capabilities to handle infrastructure changes in an elegant and controlled manner. One such capability is the lifecycle configuration block.

The lifecycle block provides several meta-arguments to manage how Terraform creates, updates, checks and deletes resources. In this post, we will dive into Terraform's lifecycle block and demonstrate its usage with a few examples with Microsoft Azure resources.

Understanding the Lifecycle Block

Note: This post was written using Terraform (v1.4.x)

The lifecycle block in Terraform provides control over how a resource is managed. It's a configuration block that is nested within a resource block and supports four meta-arguments:

resource "provider_resource" "block" { // ... some resource configuration ... lifecycle { argument = value }
}

The lifecycle block also has an option to configure custom condition checks using preconditions and postconditions:

resource "provider_resource" "block" { // ... some resource configuration ... lifecycle { precondition { condition = expression error_message = "error(string)" } postcondition { condition = expression error_message = "error(string)" } }
}

We will take a closer look at preconditions and postconditions a bit later, but let's first look at a few examples using meta-arguments:

  • create_before_destroy
  • prevent_destroy
  • ignore_changes
  • replace_triggered_by

1. Create Before Destroy

Argument Type: Boolean

In some scenarios, destroying a resource before creating a new one can lead to downtime. To circumvent this, we can set create_before_destroy to true. This can be particularly useful when working with Azure Virtual Machines or App Services, where you'd want to minimize downtime.

Here is an example with an Azure Virtual Machine:

resource "azurerm_virtual_machine" "example" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { create_before_destroy = true }
}

In this scenario, when an update is required that can't be performed in place, Terraform will first create the replacement VM and then destroy the old one, reducing potential downtime.

2. Prevent Destroy

Argument Type: Boolean

Setting prevent_destroy to true is a protective measure to prevent accidental deletion of critical resources. If you attempt to destroy such a resource, Terraform will return an error and stop the operation. This can be useful when working with Azure SQL Databases, Storage Accounts, or any resource that holds important data.

Here is an example with an Azure SQL Database:

resource "azurerm_sql_database" "example" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }
}

With this configuration, Terraform will prevent the SQL database from being accidentally destroyed.

3. Ignore Changes

Argument Type: list of attribute names

The ignore_changes argument is useful when you want to manage certain resource attributes outside of Terraform, or when you want to avoid spurious diffs.

Here is an example with an Azure App Service:

resource "azurerm_app_service" "example" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { ignore_changes = [ app_settings, // Ignore changes to app_settings attribute ] }
}

Say a different team manages an App Services app_settings for example, you may be provisioning that App Service, but the configuration is left up to someone else, or maybe even a different automation all together is taking care of the app_settings configuration, and you do not want Terraform to revert, interfere or potentially remove those settings.

In this case, any changes to the app_settings of the App Service will be ignored by Terraform.

Tip: You can also use a special value all that will ignore all settings once a resource is provisioned.

resource "azurerm_app_service" "example" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { ignore_changes = all }
}

This will provision the resource any any subsequent configuration outside of Terraform will be ignored by Terraform.

4. Replace Triggered By

Argument Type: list of resource or attribute references

The replace_triggered_by argument allows you to replace a resource when another resource changes. You can only reference managed resources in replace_triggered_by expressions. Supply a list of expressions referencing managed resources, instances, or instance attributes.

resource "azurerm_sql_database" "example" { // ... other configuration ...
} resource "azurerm_app_service" "example" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { replace_triggered_by = [ azurerm_sql_database.example.id, //Replace `azurerm_app_service` each time `azurerm_sql_database` id changes ] }
}

replace_triggered_by allows only resource addresses because the decision is based on the planned actions for all of the given resources, meaning that variables, data sources and modules are not supported.

Plain values such as local values or input variables do not have planned actions of their own, but you can treat them with a resource-like lifecycle by using them with the terraform_data resource type.

Custom Condition Checks

You can add precondition and postcondition blocks with a lifecycle block to specify assumptions and guarantees about how resources and data sources operate. The following examples creates a precondition that checks whether the AMI is properly configured.

data "azurerm_mssql_server" "example" { // ... other configuration ...
} resource "azurerm_mssql_database" "test" { // ... other configuration ... lifecycle { precondition { condition = data.azurerm_mssql_server.example.version == "12.0" error_message = "MSSQL server version incorrect (Needs to be version 12.0)." } postcondition { condition = self.transparent_data_encryption_enabled == true error_message = "The Database must have TDE enabled." } }
}

NOTE: The self object above in the postcondition block refers to attributes of the instance under evaluation (e.g. the MSSQL database).

You can implement a validation check as either a postcondition of the resource producing the data, or as a precondition of a resource or output value using the data. To decide which is most appropriate, consider whether the check is representing an assumption or a guarantee.

In our example above:

  • Assumption: Validate using preconditions that the database is being created, is on a MSSQL server that is version 12.0.
  • GUarantee: Validating using postcondition that the MSSQL database being created (SELF), has transparent_data_encryption_enabled set to true.

Conclusion

Terraform's lifecycle block provides a powerful way to control and manage your resources. Whether it's preventing accidental destruction of critical resources, managing zero-downtime updates, or ignoring changes to certain attributes, the lifecycle block offers you the flexibility you need. As always, be sure to test these configurations in a non-production environment before rolling out to production to ensure they work as expected. Happy Terraforming!

I hope you have enjoyed this post and have learned something new. ❤️

Author

Like, share, follow me on: 🐙 GitHub | 🐧 Twitter | 👾 LinkedIn
item.author: pwd9000

item.title: JS异步-技术论文 | JS Asynchronous-Technical Paper
item.description:
安吉库马尔

发表于 6 月 1 日

回调函数是作为参数传递给另一个函数的函数，旨在稍后调用，通常在异步操作或特定事件发生之后。 回调是 JavaScript 中用于处理异步代码执行的基本概念。
例子：-

 function fetchData(callback) { setTimeout(() => { const data = 'Sample data'; callback(data); }, 2000); } function processData(data) { console.log('Processing data:', data); } console.log('Start'); fetchData(processData); console.log('End');

承诺

例子：-

 function fetchData() { return new Promise((resolve, reject) => { setTimeout(() => { const data = 'Sample data'; resolve(data); // Resolving the promise with the fetched data // If there's an error, you can reject the promise instead: // reject(new Error('Failed to fetch data')); }, 2000); }); } function processData(data) { console.log('Processing data:', data); } console.log('Start'); fetchData() .then(processData) .catch(error => { console.error('Error:', error.message); }) .finally(() => { console.log('Cleanup'); }); console.log('End');

以下是执行流程：

  1. 调用fetchData函数，并创建一个新的 Promise。
  2. 异步操作从设置为 2000 毫秒的计时器开始。
  3. 使用获取的数据调用processData函数。
  4. 调用finally方法，并将“Cleanup”记录到控制台。

代码控制

事件循环在管理异步代码执行的控制流中起着至关重要的作用。 它确保任务（包括回调和事件）以特定顺序执行，同时保持 JavaScript 运行时单线程。
事件循环遵循由以下阶段组成的循环：

  1. 处理同步代码：事件循环从执行调用堆栈中存在的任何同步代码开始。 这包括执行非异步的函数、操作和语句。

  2. 处理异步操作：同步代码执行后，事件循环检查是否有任何异步操作已完成。

  • 如果有已完成的异步操作，则将它们关联的回调或承诺添加到回调队列或微任务队列（取决于任务类型）。

  • 队列中的回调或承诺不会立即执行； 他们正在等待调用堆栈为空。

  1. 事件循环迭代：如果调用堆栈为空，事件循环开始迭代以处理队列中等待的回调和承诺。

  • 事件循环首先检查微任务队列。 微任务通常包括承诺回调、变异观察者或其他高优先级任务。 它处理队列中存在的所有微任务，直到队列为空。

  • 处理完所有微任务后，事件循环移动到回调队列并开始执行回调。 这些可能是计时器回调、事件处理程序或其他优先级较低的任务。 它从队列中获取一个回调并执行它。

  • 事件循环重复这个过程，以交替的方式不断地从微任务队列和回调队列中检查和执行任务。

参考

玻璃钢

一旦暂停，codebyan 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

一旦解除暂停，codebyank 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，codebyank 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 codebyan 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 codebyank：

使 codebyan 的所有帖子不那么显眼

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 codebyan 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ankit Kumar

Posted on Jun 1

a callback function is a function that is passed as an argument to another function and is intended to be called later, often after an asynchronous operation or a certain event occurs. Callbacks are a fundamental concept in JavaScript for handling asynchronous code execution.
Example:-

function fetchData(callback) {
  setTimeout(() => {
    const data = 'Sample data';
    callback(data);
  }, 2000);
}

function processData(data) {
  console.log('Processing data:', data);
}

console.log('Start');
fetchData(processData);
console.log('End');

Promises

Promises are a feature introduced in ECMAScript 2015 (ES6) that provide a more structured and readable way to handle asynchronous operations in JavaScript. Promises represent the eventual completion (or failure) of an asynchronous operation and allow you to chain multiple asynchronous operations together.
Example:-

    function fetchData() {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    setTimeout(() => {
      const data = 'Sample data';
      resolve(data); // Resolving the promise with the fetched data
      // If there's an error, you can reject the promise instead:
      // reject(new Error('Failed to fetch data'));
    }, 2000);
  });
}

function processData(data) {
  console.log('Processing data:', data);
}

console.log('Start');
fetchData()
  .then(processData)
  .catch(error => {
    console.error('Error:', error.message);
  })
  .finally(() => {
    console.log('Cleanup');
  });
console.log('End');

Here's how the execution flows:

  1. "Start" is logged to the console.
  2. The fetchData function is called, and a new Promise is created.
  3. The asynchronous operation starts with a timer set for 2000 milliseconds.
  4. "End" is logged to the console.
  5. After 2000 milliseconds, the timer expires, and the promise is resolved with the fetched data.
  6. The then method is called on the promise, and the processData function is passed as the callback.
  7. The processData function is called with the fetched data.
  8. "Processing data: Sample data" is logged to the console.
  9. The finally method is called, and "Cleanup" is logged to the console.

Code Control

the event loop plays a crucial role in managing the control flow of asynchronous code execution. It ensures that tasks, including callbacks and events, are executed in a specific order while keeping the JavaScript runtime single-threaded.
The event loop follows a cycle that consists of the following phases:

  1. Handle Synchronous Code: The event loop starts by executing any synchronous code present in the call stack. This includes executing functions, operations, and statements that are not asynchronous.

  2. Handle Asynchronous Operations: After the synchronous code is executed, the event loop checks if there are any asynchronous operations that have completed.

  • If there are completed asynchronous operations, their associated callbacks or promises are added to the callback queue or microtask queue (depending on the type of task).

  • The callbacks or promises in the queues are not executed immediately; they are waiting for the call stack to be empty.

  1. Event Loop Iteration: If the call stack is empty, the event loop starts iterating to handle the callbacks and promises waiting in the queues.

  • The event loop checks the microtask queue first. Microtasks generally include promise callbacks, mutation observers, or other high-priority tasks. It processes all the microtasks present in the queue until it is empty.

  • After processing all the microtasks, the event loop moves to the callback queue and starts executing the callbacks. These could be timer callbacks, event handlers, or other lower-priority tasks. It takes one callback from the queue and executes it.

  • The event loop repeats this process, continuously checking and executing tasks from the microtask queue and the callback queue in an alternating manner.

References

W3SCHOOL
GFG

shrey vijayvargiya - May 17

Once suspended, codebyank will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, codebyank will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by codebyank will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If codebyank is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag codebyank:

Make all posts by codebyank less visible

codebyank consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging codebyank will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 2023 年 33 大 DevOps 监控工具 | Top 33 DevOps Monitoring Tools For 2023
item.description:
它主要关注团队授权、协作、沟通和技术自动化。 换句话说， DevOps是强调团队持续改进和协作以简化软件开发过程的工作文化。

为了在这个快速发展的数字世界中保持竞争力，传统的软件开发方法无法跟上交付高质量软件应用程序的步伐。 当我们将其与传统的软件开发方法进行比较时，运营和开发团队在孤岛中工作。 这导致了软件开发过程中的延迟、错误和沟通不畅等问题。 在这篇关于顶级 DevOps 监控工具的博客中，我们将了解有关顶级 DevOps 监控工具的更多信息，以确保应用程序的最佳性能和交付。 我们还将探索一些顶级 DevOps 监控工具的不同功能。 这将帮助您为您的软件开发项目选择合适的工具。

让我们开始吧！

什么是 DevOps？

但是具有功能组件的软件应用程序可能需要更多的工作来跟踪系统健康状况和性能。 您如何知道您的软件应用程序何时面临问题？ 您的开发团队如何掌握错误并确保无缝的用户体验？

答案是 DevOps 监控。 它提供对软件应用程序和服务性能的洞察力，使团队能够快速找到并解决问题。 使用 LambdaTest 云在 50 多种浏览器和操作系统组合上立即运行您的Playwright 自动化测试测试脚本。

什么是 DevOps 监控？

这种监控因战略到开发、集成到测试以及部署到运营而异。 通过 DevOps 监控，您可以跟踪软件应用程序、服务和基础架构状态的全面实时视图。

简单来说，DevOps 监控是指识别、跟踪、分析和报告软件开发管道的特定组件的持续操作。 此管道包括持续规划、开发、集成、测试、部署和运营。

通过 DevOps 监控，您可以提高开发团队的效率，因为它可以在将代码发布到生产环境之前轻松发现潜在问题。 以下是 DevOps 监控的其他一些意义：

  • 您可以跨 DevOps 的各个方面定义、跟踪和衡量与软件应用程序开发相关的实际关键绩效指标。

  • 这使您可以确定何时优化性能、安全性和成本以及其他方面。

  • 您可以轻松检测软件应用程序中的错误并将其报告给开发团队，以便在影响最终用户体验之前及时修复。

  • 它允许您分析日志和指标，以发现软件应用程序中问题的根本原因。 这将导致改进平均检测时间 (MTTD)、平均隔离时间 (MTTI)、平均修复时间 (MTTR) 和平均恢复时间 (MTTR)。

  • 它有助于识别任何潜在的安全威胁，并向 DevOps 团队提供必要的信息以迅速做出响应。

  • 通过监控，您可以深入了解 DevOps 团队的工作流程，从而识别需要自动化的区域，并让开发人员专注于更关键的任务。

  • 监控提供可用于识别应用程序行为模式的数据，使 DevOps 团队更容易预测未来的问题并采取主动措施来控制它们。

这有助于确保组织在开发软件应用程序的整个 DevOps 生命周期中遵循最佳实践。 现在让我们探索更多 DevOps 监控工具。

这些工具从不同来源收集数据，并提供一个显示性能、使用指标和可用性的仪表板。 然后使用此类数据来确定开发软件应用程序的趋势和问题，这些趋势和问题可以在成为重大问题之前得到解决。

他们使监控过程自动化，并提供足够的数据驱动的洞察力来修复软件应用程序中的错误。 以下是 DevOps 监控工具的其他一些重要性：

  • 它有助于确保软件应用程序的 IT 系统、性能和整体功能。

  • 工具可以实时了解应用程序、系统和服务的行为，帮助组织识别和修复问题。

到目前为止，2023 年最需要的自动化测试工具有哪些？ 让我们来看看。

前 33 名 DevOps 监控工具

选择正确的 DevOps 监控工具需要仔细考虑和专业知识。

当我们研究 DevOps 方法时， 持续测试在确保整个软件开发生命周期中的代码质量方面起着至关重要的作用。 持续测试是指在引入新代码更改时自动化测试过程并连续执行测试的做法。 在持续测试 (CT) 的上下文中，有许多框架可用于前端测试，包括 Selenium、TestNG、JUnit 等。

作为一个开源测试自动化框架，Selenium 使团队能够在整个软件开发生命周期中连续自动执行测试。 借助 Selenium，组织可以为 Web 应用程序创建自动化测试，涵盖用户交互、数据输入和预期输出等各种场景。 这些测试可以通过与持续集成和持续交付 (CI/CD)管道集成来以连续的方式执行。

虽然 Selenium 测试通常在本地机器上执行，但像 LambdaTest 这样的云平台提供了一种可扩展且方便的解决方案，可以跨各种浏览器和操作系统以分布式方式运行 Selenium 测试。

检查 Web 元素以帮助开发人员和测试人员调试 UI 缺陷或修改 HTML 或 CSS 文件。 了解如何在 Mac 上进行检查。

现在，让我们看看一些顶级的 DevOps 监控工具来为您提供帮助。

它是服务器监控工具下分类的顶级基于云的 DevOps 监控工具之一。 借助 Sensu，您可以衡量和监控软件应用程序、业务 KPI 和基础架构的功能和性能。

是什么让 Sensu 脱颖而出成为顶级 DevOps 监控工具之一？

  • 它是一个用 Ruby 编程语言编写的开源监控框架。

  • 它有一个专用的 API，其结构是通过标记许多指标的阈值来监控大型动态系统。

  • 它提供了所有云系统和协议的惊人视图，包括 Kubernetes。

  • 您可以使用自定义脚本和 Nagios 风格的插件进行详细而完整的系统和服务健康监控。

  • 提供自动修复功能，这意味着它可以在检测到任何错误或错误时自动启动服务重启以运行自定义脚本。

  • 包含许多集成，包括事件管理工具、Web 和自动化平台、数据工具和云平台。

  • 当在您的应用程序或基础架构中发现错误时，它会发送警报和通知。

  • 提供客户端的动态注册和注销。 这允许客户端自动注册到监控系统，也可以删除当前客户端。

  • 它适用于自动化流程，您可以将其配置为在服务出现故障时自动重启服务，并在满足特定条件时触发备份流程。

它为开发人员和 DevOps 团队提供了一个平台，用于自动化和监控软件应用程序的性能。

是什么让 Jenkins 脱颖而出成为顶级 DevOps 监控工具之一？

  • 它是 DevOps 团队中非常流行的工具，用于实施持续集成和交付管道。

  • 支持与其他 DevOps 工具和服务集成的各种插件。 此类插件可自动执行源代码管理等任务，从而构建完整的 DevOps 自动化管道。

  • 它是可扩展的，可以帮助开发人员使用插件和编写脚本向平台添加自定义功能。

  • 支持 CI/CD 管道监控，帮助 DevOps 团队实时跟踪部署进度。

  • 它与 LambdaTest 集成，使用此插件，您可以将 Selenium 脚本从 Jenkin CI 实例自动化到 LambdaTest。

在本教程中，了解什么是回归测试、它的重要性、类型以及如何执行它。

它是一个基于云的操作性能平台和 DevOps 监控工具，允许与操作人员密切合作以评估软件应用程序的可靠性。 换句话说，PagerDuty 为 IT 运营和 DevOps 团队提供单一系统，以有效管理警报、随叫随到调度和事件管理。

是什么让 PagerDuty 脱颖而出成为顶级 DevOps 监控工具之一？

  • 提供易于使用的事件响应和警报系统。

  • 当您在 PagerDuty 中收到警报时，如果在给定时间内未确认或解决警报，系统将自动升级警报。

  • 它有一个强大的 GUI 工具，用于设置随叫随到的时间表和升级策略。 有了这个，您可以轻松管理随叫随到的轮换，并确保在事件发生时通知开发人员。

这样的环境包括多个部门、业务单元和应用程序，所有这些都必须协同工作以实现组织的目标。

这个 DevOps 监控工具的本质目的在于自动化监控整个部署环境中的 SQL 脚本执行事件。 这种自动化工作最大限度地减少了人为错误并简化了数据库审计和部署监控。

是什么让 Datical Deployment Monitoring Console 脱颖而出成为顶级 DevOps 监控工具之一？

  • 该解决方案的主要功能之一是能够简化数据库审计。

  • 该软件通过其自动化功能，毫不费力地跟踪部署和错误，从而有助于在问题出现时进行识别和解决。

  • 该自动化软件简化了部署过程，允许用户和管理员跟踪、审核和解决可能出现的任何特定于部署的数据库问题。

在线对 3000 多个浏览器执行手动或自动跨浏览器测试。 使用最强大的跨浏览器在线测试工具更快地部署和扩展。

此类工具的示例包括问题跟踪器、敏捷规划工具、源代码存储库、构建系统和测试自动化框架。 它是一个一站式解决方案，可管理所有软件交付集成需求，但不包括对其他工具的需求。

简单来说，您可以在正确的时间向开发人员提供准确的信息，并使用正确的工具和界面。 该工具允许开发团队有效地联系和协作。 因此，它通过自动化各种工具和系统之间的工作流程和信息来提高生产力。

是什么让 Tasktop Integration Hub 脱颖而出成为顶级 DevOps 监控工具之一？

  • 支持与 45 种不同的工具集成，这些工具功能齐全，无需额外配置和设置。

  • 对于现有的集成，Tasktop Integration Hub 还可以添加新的工具。

  • 它允许您在过滤器的基础上路由工件和字段更新，符合与频率和方向相关的最终用户要求。

  • 您可以通过基于 Web 的界面安全登录，确保只有授权用户才能访问敏感数据。

  • 它以低占用空间运行，并降低了软件开发过程中其他工具的负载。 这有助于确保集成顺利进行，而不会减慢其他流程。

  • 提供软件开发生命周期的实时可见性，因为它使信息流自动化，并允许您监控进度并找到任何数据驱动的决策。

  • 提供从需求到代码再到部署的端到端测试。

  • 通过提供开发过程的统一视图来促进实时协作。

中间件是为云原生环境设计的可观察性平台，具备基础设施监控能力。 该工具为 DevOps 团队提供实时可见性，使他们能够监控本地和云部署。

借助中间件，团队可以通过密切监控各种组件（例如应用程序、流程、服务器、容器、事件、数据库等）来评估其基础设施的整体健康状况。 通过提供全面的洞察力和监控功能，中间件可帮助 DevOps 团队有效地管理和维护其基础设施。

它是一种 DevOps 监控工具，可以更好地洞察系统、应用程序和基础架构的性能和健康状况。 您可以将 Librato 理解为一种监控服务，它使用来自任何来源的指标提供实时分析。 它允许聚合、转换和关联来自不同来源（如服务器、数据库、负载平衡器、Web 应用程序、移动应用程序和自定义业务应用程序）的指标。

是什么让 Librato 脱颖而出成为顶级 DevOps 监控工具之一？

  • 您可以将自定义基础架构、业务指标和应用程序转化为洞察力，这有助于做出数据驱动的决策来优化系统。

  • 您可以通过 Librato 获得基础架构的单屏视图。

  • 您可以轻松识别并解决问题。

  • 它允许通过其简化的服务级别和跟踪级别的根本原因摘要快速识别和解决应用程序的问题。

  • 提供服务，例如收集和分析来自不同来源的指标，以及提供图形、图表和仪表板等可视化效果。 它有助于应用程序的数据监控。

  • 当您完成不同指标（CPU 使用率、内存使用率、网络流量等）的活动处理时，Librato 会提供实时通知。

  • 您不必安装 Librato，这样可以很容易地开始使用它。

  • 从 Librato 收到的警报是可靠的，可为用户提供有关其生产环境中潜在问题的准确信息。

它提供了一个包含警报管理器的综合解决方案，使其成为检测和解决系统相关问题的有效工具。

随着它越来越受欢迎，越来越多的企业和组织将 Prometheus 集成到他们的基础设施中。 用户和开发人员社区的这种协作努力导致了该工具的不断改进和更新。

是什么让 Prometheus 脱颖而出成为顶级 DevOps 监控工具之一？

  • 它是一款出色的端到端监控工具，带有警报管理器。

  • 它可以与 Grafana 等 DevOps 监控工具集成，以实现可视化和警报管理。

  • 它基于 PromQL 查询语言，这是一种“只读”语言，可为查询指标提供灵活性。

  • 使用推送网关来支持 HAProxy、StatsD、Graphite 等导出器。

  • 它旨在满足现代大型基础设施的需求，提供用于存储时间序列数据、实时图形、查询收集的数据以及根据指定条件发出警报的预构建组件。

  • 不依赖于分布式存储，而是构建为在单个服务器节点上工作。

  • 附带默认库和服务器，可用于不同的操作系统和应用程序，如 Windows、Linux 和 MySQL。

  • 它可以存储过去的事件以进行长期分析，而不会丢失分辨率或细节。 此操作允许用户访问历史数据，并利用对系统性能随时间推移的趋势和模式的洞察。

  • 没有任何依赖性，并提供用于自定义开发的 Web API。

  • 您可以使用以 Go、Java、Python 和 Ruby 等不同编程语言提供的 Prometheus Client 库向代码添加检测。

开发人员利用此工具与 Elasticsearch 合作构建软件应用程序并记录来自各种来源的数据。 最初，Kibana 是作为 Elastic 的开源项目开发的。 它旨在提供一个用户界面，允许最终用户探索 Elasticsearch 查询并实时登录。 但是，目前，许多组织将其用作独立的 DevOps 监控工具来满足分析需求。

是什么让 Kibana 脱颖而出成为顶级 DevOps 监控工具之一？

  • 它具有强大的查询能力，例如聚合和过滤数据。

  • 您可以创建个性化的可视化效果，例如折线图、条形图和饼图，它们可以进一步定制以满足特定需求。

  • 您可以轻松快速地设置 Kibana，从而开始使用该工具。

  • 它允许用户查看有助于轻松识别和修复软件应用程序生产中的问题的数据。

  • 它具有自动突出显示功能，可帮助用户识别其日志文件中的潜在问题。 您会得到突出显示的搜索字段，这些字段可能会显示问题或错误，并使您能够及时修复。

  • 您可以可视化日志文件并以图形显示必要的数据和实时统计数据，以便轻松理解数据。

  • 集中式监控集群允许 Kibana 监控和比较 Elastic Stack 的众多部署（包括 Elasticsearch、Kibana、Beats 和 Logstash），从而促进跨不同部署的数据的直接评估。

  • 您可以在 Kibana 中自由配置保留策略，这决定了数据在存储中保留多长时间。 此功能有助于管理保留数据的数量，并确保在不再需要时丢弃过时的数据。

  • 它是一种复杂的 DevOps 监控工具，尤其适用于分析机器数据。 生成的此类数据使用基于网络的界面，该界面允许将相关数据纳入中央索引，这有助于轻松找到相关信息。

    是什么让 Splunk 脱颖而出成为顶级 DevOps 监控工具之一？

    • 使用 Splunk，您可以检查来自网络、服务器和应用程序的数据。

    • 它能够仔细检查来自不同来源（例如网络、服务器和应用程序）的数据，使其成为监视机器生成数据的全方位解决方案。

    • 它在生产环境中的轻松部署伴随着 Splunk light 功能，该功能负责将数据从多个服务器传输到主要 Splunk 引擎进行分析。

    • 该工具以一种能够产生强大分析洞察力的方式对数据进行索引，使组织能够以无与伦比的准确性确定需要改进的领域。

      16. Sumo Logic

      Sumo Logic is an open-source DevOps monitoring tool particularly used for monitoring servers, applications, and infrastructure components, analyzing and alerting data from different sources. It was initially developed by Sensu Inc in 2012, and now it has become one of the most used DevOps monitoring tools.

      Sumo Logic can be leveraged by integrating with unified digital experience platforms like LambdaTest. It provides scalable, secure, and reliable test orchestration for end-users at different stages in the DevOps Life cycle. The LambdaTest integration with Sumo Logic allows real-time visibility on key metrics and supports usage and test data analytics. With this, you will have comprehensive insights into the software application development process and enable the team to make informed decisions for faster release.

      What makes Sumo Logic stand out as one of the top DevOps monitoring tools?

      • It allows the DevOps team to monitor the working of the microservice-based applications through a single platform, which includes performance metrics, logs, and event data.

      • Monitors diverse system and software application checks like HTTP checks, TCP checks, and log analysis. It provides process monitoring that shows the functionality and performance of the software infrastructure.

      • Monitors cloud security and business intelligence features.

      • Automatically detect new services and infrastructure and display them in pre-configured dashboards. It will help you get a quick overview of software applications.

      • Provides service maps that help you see how diverse components of software applications are associated and depend on each other.

      • Monitors user interaction with your software application and detects any issue in the page slowing it down.

      • Utilizes machine learning algorithms to find anomalies in the software application and automatically detect any root cause.

      • Provides a database of performance metrics for known software stacks that help compare software application performance to industry standards and best practices.

      • Provides pre-built dashboards and visualization for different use cases that help detect any issue within your software applications.

      17. Dynatrace

      Dynatrace is a comprehensive DevOps monitoring tool that can meticulously test software application logs without any negative impact or clashes on the server. Dynatrace's exquisite design allows it to operate within the application process while not exceeding the 10 MB memory consumption threshold, reducing server overhead.

      Further, it is non-reliant on server restarts to include or exclude agents from application servers, significantly saving valuable time and resources.

      What makes Dynatrace stand out as one of the top DevOps monitoring tools?

      • The various capabilities of Dynatrace extend beyond test logs ; it can effectively yield valuable insights into the performance of an application. For instance, it can illustrate the duration of each phase of an application's execution and provide a transactional flow.

      • It can also explicitly highlight any issues or errors in production workflows.

      • It identifies deviations from standard benchmarks, detects any unusual activity within the application or network, and promptly alerts users.

      • Offer a clear and concise overview of the software application's performance, making it accessible and understandable to even non-technical users.

      • Enables the monitoring of multiple facets within a unified platform, such as real users, applications, clouds, servers, networks, and infrastructure.

      • Provide effortless deployment process and automated dependency discovery, which considerably saves time and mitigates the possibility of errors during deployment.

      • Its versatility is demonstrated by its support for various technologies such as WebUI, Java, node.js, and .NET-based applications.

      18. Grafana

      Grafana is a DevOps monitoring tool and open-source analytics and interactive visualization platform. Grafana is known to support data presentation methods utilizing pluggable panel architecture. You can use this tool in a DevOps environment to monitor and analyze data from different sources.

      What makes Grafana stand out as one of the top DevOps monitoring tools?

      • It has an alerting feature, and Grafana is integrated with other alerting systems like Alertmanager.

      • Utilizes a user-friendly interface that helps end-users to create, edit and share dashboards.

      • It comes with dashboard templating, allowing customization of dashboard setup to suit every need.

      • It boasts the capability to generate annotations on graphs and acquire data from a broad range of data sources.

      • It has the functionality to enable kiosk mode and playlist options, which can be used to exhibit dashboards on TV screens and conceal extraneous interface components.

      • It has extended functionalities with plugins like Zabbix, Influx Admin Panel, etc.

      • It includes straightforward-to-code alert hooks, which enable the creation of custom notifiers for a diverse array of notifications and alerts.

      19. Zabbix

      It is an open-source DevOps monitoring tool and solution for IT components like software application networks, servers, virtual machines, and cloud services. Zabbix also provides monitoring services for different end-users in a multi-tenant environment.

      What makes Zabbix stand out as one of the top DevOps monitoring tools?

      • Zabbix can gather data from any source at custom intervals. Uses backend database values to determine any flexible issue threshold in the software applications. For example, when certain conditions are met, like when the server's CPU usage goes beyond a certain threshold, you get a notification.

      • Based on the escalation schedule, recipients, and media types, Zabbix customizes sending notifications. It helps you ensure the correct user is notified at the right time.

      • It allows you to store data over time which can be accessed easily at any time for reporting and analysis.
